title: Public Linux A733 dependency and clock-source audit

findings.finding: Public search still resolves the A733 CCU/PRCM dependency to Junhui Liu's RFC series 20260310-a733-clk-v1-0-36b4e9b24457@pigmoral.tech.
findings.decision: Keep Depends-on tracking for this RFC; do not submit competing local CCU scaffolding.

findings.finding: Public search still resolves the A733 pinctrl dependency to Andre Przywara's RFC series 20250821004232.8134-1-andre.przywara@arm.com.
findings.decision: Keep Depends-on tracking for this RFC; do not submit competing local pinctrl scaffolding.

findings.finding: Newer A733 v2 hits are U-Boot-side support, including A733 SoC, clock/reset, pinctrl, and MMC enablement.
findings.decision: Do not treat U-Boot v2 patch numbering as a Linux dependency replacement.

findings.finding: The diagnostic Linux tree already carries optional SDMMC0 store, MBUS, and MSI-lite clock consumers, and prior tests did not fix normal-IDMAC descriptor fetch. Public U-Boot/source evidence does not name a new untested MMC0 gate/reset offset.
findings.decision: No new behavior patch is justified from clock/reset evidence.

findings.finding: Public search did not yet find an archive hit for the A733 IDMAC descriptor-read question.
findings.decision: Continue monitoring; absence of archive proof is not a reason to resend immediately.

conclusion: No source-backed runtime delta was found. The next kernel behavior patch still requires maintainer/list feedback, a public/vendor source clue naming a specific untested register/reset/fabric bit, or a deliberately labeled watch-only diagnostic promoted by stronger evidence.
